ai_strategy_lead.name: Shishir Shah
ai_strategy_lead.title: Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer; Director, School of Computer Science, Gallogly College of Engineering

documents.title: AI Roadmaps - Office of the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer
Announcing OU's Next Phase of AI Innovation (Provost memo, Aug 12 2025)
OU Enters Next Phase of AI Innovation (news announcement)

ai_strategy.summary: OU positions AI as a pillar of its 'Lead On, University: The Next Phase' strategic plan. In August 2025, the provost named Shishir Shah, Ph.D., as OU's first university-wide Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer (pending Regents approval), reporting jointly to the Norman and Health Sciences provosts. Shah also leads the Gallogly School of Computer Science. The Office of the CAIO has published AI Roadmaps covering research, education, healthcare, and infrastructure implementation, with a Healthcare Implementation Working Group running structured rollouts from summer through early fall 2025. Roughly 25% of OU's research already involves AI, and the university frames its AI agenda around curriculum redesign so graduates are 'literate and responsible AI-users.'

key_provisions: Public versions of generative AI tools are not appropriate for OU institutional or sensitive data; users must comply with the Acceptable Use Policy for University Data
Microsoft Copilot with organizational data protection (signed in with OU ID) is the recommended generative AI tool for OU faculty and staff
Five PolicyStat policy documents cover administrative/operational use, student guidance, governance, teaching, and research
Each course sets its own policies for AI use; Center for Faculty Excellence provides sample syllabus language and faculty workshops
Microsoft 365 Copilot is an optional service being evaluated through a 3-phase enterprise pilot
Kaltura MyMedia AI captions do not currently satisfy ADA accuracy requirements; ADA-compliant captions are obtained through a contracted vendor
ai_policy.summary: OU's AI policy posture is cautious-comprehensive: responsible, ethical, and transparent use is encouraged across teaching, research, and operations, with hard limits on using public GenAI for institutional or sensitive data. Microsoft Copilot (web) with organizational data protection is the officially recommended tool. Course-level AI rules are delegated to individual faculty, supported by Center for Faculty Excellence guidance and workshops rather than a campus-wide syllabus mandate.

bodies.name: Office of the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer
bodies.scope: University-wide AI strategy, policy, and roadmap; coordinates across Norman and Health Sciences campuses

bodies.name: Healthcare Implementation Working Group
bodies.scope: Structured AI implementation in OU Health Sciences from summer through early fall 2025

bodies.name: Data Institute for Societal Challenges (DISC)
bodies.scope: Coordinates convergent AI/data-science research teams and seed funding across 6+ communities of practice

bodies.name: Center for Faculty Excellence - AI Policies and Resources
bodies.scope: Faculty-facing AI policy guidance, teaching workshops, sample syllabus language

bodies.name: Oklahoma State Regents AI Impact in Higher Education Committee
bodies.scope: System/state-level committee (35+ members) studying AI across Oklahoma higher education; OU participates

governance_structure.summary: OU's AI governance centers on the newly created Office of the Chief AI Officer (Shishir Shah, August 2025), which sits above a set of working groups including the Healthcare Implementation Working Group and research coordination through DISC. The Center for Faculty Excellence houses the PolicyStat-based AI policies and teaching-focused guidance. OU also sits on Oklahoma's system-wide AI Impact in Higher Education Committee established in May 2023 - one of the earliest state-level AI higher-ed committees in the U.S.

initiatives.title: Appointment of inaugural university-wide Chief AI Officer
initiatives.description: In August 2025, OU named Shishir Shah, Ph.D. as its first university-wide Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer (pending Regents approval). Shah, formerly chair of Computer Science at the University of Houston and founder of its Quantitative Imaging Laboratory, will concurrently direct OU's School of Computer Science and report jointly to the Norman and Health Sciences provosts.
initiatives.significance: Establishing a cross-campus CAIO role with joint reporting lines signals a coordinated, enterprise-scale AI governance model rather than siloed college-level efforts.

investment.description: Executive hire and new cross-campus office; salary and staffing undisclosed

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a single accountable executive for AI policy, risk, and roadmap execution across the OU system.

initiatives.title: $200K seed funding for 20 AI pilot projects
initiatives.description: In June 2025, DISC awarded nearly $200,000 in pilot seed funding to 20 faculty/staff-led AI projects spanning clinical imaging automation, hospital readmission prediction, dental imaging, faculty development using Bloom's Taxonomy, legal education, traffic engineering, critical AI literacy instruction, LLM fine-tuning platforms, data preprocessing tools for non-programmers, responsible AI curriculum, and multi-agent fact-checking. Grants ranged from ~$4,200 to $10,000 with deliverables due by end of August 2025.
initiatives.significance: Shows a bias toward fast, distributed experimentation across colleges rather than top-down flagship projects - a model increasingly emulated at mid-size R1s.

investment.funding_source: OU Data Institute for Societal Challenges (DISC)
investment.description: Seed grants of $4,200-$10,000 to 20 faculty/staff teams

collaborations.description: Projects span multiple OU colleges and the Health Sciences Center

initiatives.policy_implications: Signals institutional appetite for broad, distributed AI experimentation under DISC oversight.

initiatives.title: ScissorTAIL Enclave - Oklahoma's first HIPAA-compliant AI computing enclave
initiatives.description: The OU Health Sciences Center received a $500,000 Oklahoma State Regents Innovations with AI grant (approved December 8, 2025) to build 'The ScissorTAIL Enclave: Trusted AI for a Learning Healthcare System' - Oklahoma's first regulated, HIPAA-compliant computing enclave using GPU-accelerated servers. The infrastructure enables secure, high-performance training of AI models on sensitive patient data for clinical research and professional training.
initiatives.significance: Establishes a statewide reference model for HIPAA-compliant AI infrastructure that peer academic medical centers can emulate.

investment.funding_source: Oklahoma State Regents for Higher Education - Innovations with AI Program
investment.description: State-funded grant for HIPAA-compliant GPU enclave

partners: Oklahoma State Regents for Higher Education
collaborations.description: State regents grant to academic medical center

initiatives.policy_implications: Operationalizes HIPAA compliance as a precondition for clinical AI training, rather than after-the-fact.

initiatives.title: NSF AI Institute for Research on Trustworthy AI in Weather, Climate, and Coastal Oceanography (AI2ES)
initiatives.description: OU leads AI2ES, one of the inaugural NSF National AI Research Institutes, with a $20 million NSF award. Directed by Amy McGovern (School of Meteorology and School of Computer Science), AI2ES develops trustworthy AI for extreme weather prediction (tornadoes, hail), climate forecasting, and coastal oceanography. The institute partners with academia, industry, government, and minority-serving institutions including Del Mar College.
initiatives.significance: OU's flagship federal AI institute; one of the earliest and largest NSF AI Institute awards and a cornerstone of OU's research reputation in AI for science.

investment.description: $20M multi-year award to OU-led institute with 11+ partner organizations

initiatives.policy_implications: Models trustworthy-AI principles (bias correction, transparency) at national institute scale.

initiatives.title: Center for Creativity and Authenticity in AI Cultural Production
initiatives.description: OU launched the Center for Creativity and Authenticity in AI Cultural Production in September 2024 with a $498,129 three-year grant from the U.S. National Endowment for the Humanities. Directed by Hunter Heyck (History of Science), the center spans 20 researchers in six colleges and examines trust, appropriation, authenticity, and plagiarism in AI-generated culture across six interdisciplinary teams.
initiatives.significance: One of only a handful of NEH-funded humanities AI centers in the U.S.; signals institutional investment in non-STEM AI scholarship and ethics.

investment.funding_source: U.S. National Endowment for the Humanities
investment.description: Three-year NEH grant for humanities-led AI research center

collaborations.description: Federal humanities agency + interdisciplinary OU teams

initiatives.policy_implications: Positions humanities-informed ethics and authenticity research as a peer to technical AI research.

initiatives.title: Bachelor of Science in Applied Artificial Intelligence (OU Polytechnic)
initiatives.description: OU Polytechnic Institute began accepting applications in September 2024 for a new Bachelor of Science in Applied Artificial Intelligence. The 60-credit degree-completion program runs two years on the OU-Tulsa campus (with a four-year track on the Norman campus) and emphasizes machine learning, deep learning, reinforcement learning, NLP/LLMs, and explicit ethical/societal impact training. Estimated starting salary: ~$85,000. Oklahoma State Regents approved the program in May 2024.
initiatives.significance: Among the first named 'Applied AI' bachelor's degrees at a public R1 in the south-central U.S., with an explicit degree-completion pathway for non-traditional students.

investment.funding_source: Institutional; tech-purchase stipends up to $1,000 per student
investment.description: New degree program in OU Polytechnic Institute; scholarship support available

partners: Oklahoma State Regents for Higher Education
collaborations.description: Regionally aligned degree developed with local/state employer needs in mind

initiatives.policy_implications: Embeds ethics, fairness, transparency, and societal impact as core program outcomes.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot with organizational data protection as recommended GenAI tool
initiatives.description: OU IT has designated Microsoft Copilot (web) with organizational data protection as the recommended generative AI tool for faculty and staff when signed in with OU credentials. Microsoft 365 Copilot is being evaluated through a three-phase enterprise pilot to test cost-effectiveness before a broader rollout. Public GenAI tools are explicitly prohibited for institutional or sensitive data.
initiatives.significance: Exemplifies the common R1 pattern of routing enterprise GenAI through Microsoft's data-protected Copilot stack while running M365 Copilot as a separate paid pilot.

investment.description: Licensed through existing Microsoft agreement; M365 Copilot pilot incremental

collaborations.description: Vendor partnership using Microsoft Copilot with enterprise data protection

initiatives.policy_implications: Acts as a data-protection guardrail: approved tools with enterprise agreements only; public tools banned for institutional data.

initiatives.title: OneOCII-RAML GPU cluster for AI/ML research
initiatives.description: OU's OSCER Supercomputing Center received an NSF CC* Compute award (OAC-2201561, approximately $400K) to build the OneOklahoma Cyberinfrastructure Initiative Research Accelerator for Machine Learning (OneOCII-RAML). The 7-node cluster hosts 28 NVIDIA HGX A100 GPUs (with a follow-on acquisition targeting 10 NVIDIA H100 GPUs) and serves 18 active AI/ML research teams plus 51 aspiring teams across Oklahoma. DISC has also acquired additional CPU/GPU nodes and large-scale OURdisk storage for OSCER.
initiatives.significance: Builds shared statewide AI compute that smaller Oklahoma institutions can access, extending R1 infrastructure into a regional shared-resource model.

investment.funding_source: NSF Office of Advanced Cyberinfrastructure + EPSCoR
investment.description: CC* Compute grant; NVIDIA A100/H100 GPUs and servers

collaborations.description: NSF CC* Compute + OneOCII institutions + NVIDIA hardware

initiatives.title: $2.4M NSF grants for AI consulting and 400G research network
initiatives.description: In November 2025, OU IT was awarded $2,445,829 across two NSF grants led by Henry Neeman (OSCER executive director): OneOCII-AIC ($2538234) provides statewide AI consulting to 90+ research teams at 9 institutions (60+ at OU) and builds a workforce training program, while OFFN-400 ($2503204) upgrades OU-Norman's research network to 400 Gbps - a 400% capacity increase. Both projects began October 1, 2025.
initiatives.significance: Pairs the consulting capacity problem (human facilitators) with the data-mobility problem (400G backbone) - two of the most common bottlenecks for scaling AI research across an R1 and its state partners.

investment.description: Two NSF grants: OneOCII-AIC and OFFN-400

collaborations.description: Federal-led statewide AI consulting + campus network upgrade

initiatives.title: Membership in U.S. AI Safety Institute Consortium (AISIC)
initiatives.description: In February 2024, OU joined the U.S. AI Safety Institute Consortium, led by the U.S. Department of Commerce's National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST). Representing OU: David Ebert (DISC director, Gallogly Chair in ECE) and Amy McGovern (AI2ES director). OU contributes to 20 expertise domains including human-AI teaming, AI governance, system design, and responsible AI.
initiatives.significance: Places OU among a select group of research universities formally shaping federal AI safety and evaluation standards.

investment.description: Membership in NIST AI Safety Institute Consortium

initiatives.policy_implications: OU researchers directly influence national AI safety evaluation frameworks.

initiatives.title: Google AI for Education Accelerator (first cohort)
initiatives.description: In August 2025, OU and Oklahoma State University were named to the first cohort of the Google AI for Education Accelerator, part of Google's $1B three-year commitment to U.S. education. Students get no-cost access to Google Career Certificates and AI training courses, and all Oklahoma college students receive a free 12-month Google AI Pro plan. The accelerator accompanies Google's $9B Oklahoma data-center and infrastructure investment.
initiatives.significance: Direct industry-academic AI workforce pipeline tied to Google's regional cloud/AI infrastructure investment - a template for other Gulf/Plains states.

investment.description: $9B two-year Oklahoma infrastructure + $1B three-year national education commitment; per-student AI Pro access

collaborations.description: Industry-led cohort with OU and OSU plus workforce partners

initiatives.policy_implications: Extends vendor reach deep into Oklahoma higher-ed curriculum and career services.

initiatives.title: SoonerBot student-services chatbot
initiatives.description: OU deployed SoonerBot - an Ivy.ai-powered student-services chatbot - to answer inquiries across admissions, advising, and campus offices with skills-based routing (graduate-school applicants to graduate advisors, undergraduate applicants to their advisors) and after-hours contact-form fallback. The bot contributed to OU's then-largest-ever freshman class in Fall 2019 and continues to support enrollment growth.
initiatives.significance: An early, long-running R1 deployment of AI-assisted admissions/advising chat that has persisted across multiple enrollment cycles.

initiatives.title: AI for critical literacy and faculty pedagogy pilots
initiatives.description: As part of the 20 DISC pilots, OU funded an 'Instructional Resources to Promote OU Students' Critical AI Literacy' project ($9,198, Jacob Pleasants and Julianna Kershen) and an 'AI-Enhanced Teaching: A Bloom's Taxonomy-Based Faculty Development Initiative' ($10,000, Javeed Kittur). Center for Faculty Excellence runs synchronous and writing workshops for cross-disciplinary AI discussion and a September 2024 Symposium on Learning, Assessment and Teaching Excellence.
initiatives.significance: Operationalizes AI literacy and AI-resilient assessment at the pedagogical-practice level rather than only as policy language.

investment.funding_source: DISC seed + Center for Faculty Excellence operating budget
investment.description: Two seed grants plus workshop series

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides practical syllabus-language templates and examples rather than a campus-wide mandate.

initiatives.title: 26-week Online AI & Machine Learning Bootcamp (OU Outreach)
initiatives.description: OU Outreach runs a 26-week part-time online AI & Machine Learning Bootcamp covering advanced machine learning, deep learning, NLP, GenAI, Agentic AI, and MLOps, with ~20+ hours/week of study, career coaching, and a capstone project mirroring enterprise challenges. Classes run Monday/Wednesday/Thursday.
initiatives.significance: Continuing-education AI credentialing for working adults complements the Polytechnic Institute's degree pathway.

collaborations.description: Third-party bootcamp operator delivering OU-branded program

computing_infrastructure.summary: OU's OSCER operates Schooner (346.9 TFLOPs peak, 499 compute nodes, 10,180 CPU cores, 23 TB RAM, 450 TB usable disk) and the OneOCII-RAML GPU cluster with 28 NVIDIA HGX A100 GPUs across 7 nodes, with a follow-on acquisition targeting 10 NVIDIA H100 GPUs. DISC has added additional CPU/GPU nodes and large-scale OURdisk storage. The Norman research network is being upgraded to 400 Gbps under NSF award OAC-2503204 (October 2025 start). OU Health Sciences is building the ScissorTAIL HIPAA-compliant GPU enclave ($500K state grant, 2025).

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: OneOCII-AIC statewide AI consulting
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Extends AI consulting and workforce training to 9 Oklahoma institutions, including smaller and rural campuses, to reduce geographic/digital divides in AI research access.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: AI2ES minority-serving partners (Del Mar College)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: AI2ES partners with Del Mar College, whose graduates are 80% Hispanic, to develop AI occupational skills awards.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: ADA-compliant captioning beyond Kaltura auto-captions
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: OU contracts with an external vendor for ADA-compliant captions because Kaltura MyMedia AI captions do not meet ADA accuracy thresholds.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: OU Polytechnic tech stipend
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: OUPI students in Applied AI can receive up to $1,000 in stipends for required technology (laptops, software) to reduce access barriers.

regulatory_context.summary: Oklahoma established one of the earliest U.S. state-level higher-ed AI committees in May 2023 via the Oklahoma State Regents for Higher Education (35+ members; the 'AI Impact in Higher Education Committee'). Governor Stitt's AI task force delivered final recommendations in January 2024 to make Oklahoma a top state for responsible AI, including an AI oversight committee spanning branches of government. In 2025 the Legislature considered HB1916 (responsible deployment, AI Council, regulatory sandbox, workforce program), HB1899 (stalled 'Artificial Intelligence Act of 2025'), and signed HB1364 prohibiting nonconsensual AI-generated sexual images (deepfakes). The State Regents approved $3.4M in Innovations with AI grants across 7 institutions in December 2025, including $500K to OUHSC for the ScissorTAIL enclave.

narrative_summary: The University of Oklahoma has moved from distributed AI research strength into a coordinated, enterprise-scale AI posture over the 2024-2025 period. Its August 2025 appointment of Shishir Shah, Ph.D., as inaugural university-wide Chief AI Officer - jointly reporting to the Norman and Health Sciences provosts while also directing the School of Computer Science - establishes a single accountable executive for AI strategy, policy, and roadmap. That office sits above an ecosystem that predates it: the NSF AI Institute AI2ES ($20M, weather/climate/oceanography, led by Amy McGovern) is one of the earliest national NSF AI institutes; DISC coordinates convergent AI/data-science research; OSCER runs Schooner plus the OneOCII-RAML GPU cluster (28 A100s, with H100s queued); and the 2024 NEH-funded Center for Creativity and Authenticity in AI Cultural Production anchors humanities-led AI ethics work. Policy is cautious-comprehensive: public GenAI is banned for institutional data, Microsoft Copilot with organizational data protection is the recommended tool, M365 Copilot is in a three-phase enterprise pilot, and five PolicyStat documents cover administrative, student, governance, teaching, and research use. Course-level AI rules are delegated to faculty and supported by the Center for Faculty Excellence through workshops and sample syllabus language rather than by a campus mandate. Teaching and workforce investments are substantial: a 2024-launched B.S. in Applied AI (OU Polytechnic, Tulsa and Norman), graduate certificates and degrees through DSAI, an online M.S. in Applied Computing, a 26-week AI/ML bootcamp through OU Outreach, and first-cohort membership in the Google AI for Education Accelerator (tied to Google's $9B Oklahoma infrastructure commitment). Statewide infrastructure is maturing: OneOCII-AIC/OFFN-400 ($2.4M NSF, 2025) extends AI consulting across nine Oklahoma institutions and upgrades OU-Norman's network to 400 Gbps, and the OUHSC ScissorTAIL enclave ($500K state grant, December 2025) establishes Oklahoma's first HIPAA-compliant AI computing environment. SoonerBot (Ivy.ai) has run student-facing admissions/advising chat since 2019. Gaps remain: OU has no publicly posted quantitative campus-wide AI adoption metrics, the PolicyStat policy content itself is behind login, and there is no published AI literacy requirement at the undergraduate general-education level. OU's AI posture is best characterized as research-rich, infrastructure-investing, policy-cautious, and just beginning its first coordinated enterprise-scale governance push.
